text.title: Tax Compliance and Filing Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-tax-compliance` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Lock IRS quarterly due dates (April 15, June 15, September 15, January 15) in the operator's calendar at year-start. Missing a date for any reason triggers a late-payment penalty even with safe-harbor compliance.
rule.rationale: The penalty for late payment accrues regardless of whether the safe-harbor amount is correct. Calendar locks remove the dominant failure mode (forgetting).

rule.statement: Maintain a structured record-keeping system (accounting software or ledger spreadsheet) with monthly close. Shoebox-of-receipts or 'I'll sort it at year-end' approaches are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Year-end sorting under deadline produces both lost deductions and inaccurate filings. Monthly close keeps records audit-ready throughout the year.

rule.statement: Run a structured year-end deduction sweep in November-December: retirement contributions, Section 179 equipment, expense acceleration, charitable contributions. Skipping the sweep two years in a row is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Year-end sweep captures deductions that disappear at year-end (Section 179 placed-in-service date; SEP-IRA deadline). Skipping it permanently loses the deduction window.

rule.statement: File the annual return on time, OR file an extension AND pay the estimated balance by April 15. Extension without payment is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Extension extends the time to file, NOT the time to pay. Filing extension without payment accrues late-payment penalty + interest from April 15.

rule.statement: For non-US operators, replace the IRS-specific items with the local equivalent (HMRC / Finanças / DPS) and update due-dates and safe-harbor logic. Applying IRS rules to non-US operators is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Tax compliance is jurisdiction-specific. Copy-pasting IRS rules into a non-US workflow misses local deadlines and rules entirely.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
